## 🔒 Security validation — docker-ptf rebuilt on latest master + this change

**Base:** latest `origin/master` (`2005cfada`) with this commit cherry-picked
**Go toolchain:** **1.25.11** (was 1.25.10) — builds `grpcurl` and `gnmic` from source
**Scanner:** `trivy image docker-ptf:latest --scanners vuln --ignore-unfixed`
**Image:** `docker-ptf:latest` (Debian 12.14)

### Trivy result — fixable vulnerabilities

| Target | Type | Fixable vulns | Notes |
|--------|------|:---:|-------|
| `docker-ptf:latest` (debian 12.14) | debian | **0** | OS layer clean (fixable) |
| `usr/local/bin/grpcurl` | gobinary | **0** | **stdlib v1.25.11** ✅ |
| `usr/local/bin/gnmic` | gobinary | **0** | **stdlib v1.25.11** ✅ |
| Python packages (all) | python-pkg | **0** | clean |
| `usr/local/bin/influxdb3` | — | **0** | InfluxDB v3 = Rust binary (not Go-scanned) |
| **TOTAL** | | **0** | |

### Targeted CVEs — remediated ✅

| Advisory | CVE | Component | Fixed in | Status |
|----------|-----|-----------|:---:|:---:|
| GO-2026-5037 | CVE-2026-27145 | `crypto/x509` (VerifyHostname DoS) | Go 1.25.11 | ✅ Gone |
| GO-2026-5038 | CVE-2026-42504 | `mime` (header decode DoS) | Go 1.25.11 | ✅ Gone |
| GO-2026-5039 | CVE-2026-42507 | `net/textproto` (error injection) | Go 1.25.11 | ✅ Gone |

Both source-built Go binaries (`grpcurl`, `gnmic`) now embed **stdlib v1.25.11**, confirming the toolchain bump remediates the flagged S360 / AzSecPack findings. The rebuilt image reports **0 fixable vulnerabilities**.

Updated to use flat `SYSTEM_DEFAULTS` list entries as suggested:

- `SYSTEM_DEFAULTS|swss_zmq` with `status: enabled/disabled`
- `SYSTEM_DEFAULTS|async_rec` with `status: enabled/disabled`

Removed the `route_performance` container from YANG — these entries now use the existing `SYSTEM_DEFAULTS_LIST` pattern (same as `tunnel_qos_remap`, `dhcp_server`, etc.). Force-pushed with the updated implementation.

> @deepak-singhal0408 Why can't we have these options in constants.yml? These are knobs for how internally the components work and why should this be in user configuration. We can have this disabled by default and enable it during testing by updating constants.yml. Once things stablize we can change the constants file to enable this option

 @dgsudarshan thanks for your comment. Having runtime knob for these changes will be highly desirable actually for the following reasons..
 Note: This PR just refactors the existing knobs from DEVICE_METADATA->SYSTEMS_DEFAULT, and consolidates based on their purpose/
 
  1. Runtime kill switch — if ZMQ causes issues in production, operators can disable it immediately (hset SYSTEM_DEFAULTS|swss_zmq status disabled + config reload) without needing an image rebuild or manual file editing at scale.
  2. Device-role selectivity — in a datacenter fabric, high-scale spine/aggregation switches benefit from ZMQ, while low-scale leaf/ToR switches may not need it. CONFIG_DB lets the provisioning system push different settings per device role without building separate images.
  3. Canary rollout — we can enable on a subset of devices first and expand gradually, which is important for a feature that changes the orchagent↔syncd communication path.
  4. Consistency — synchronous_mode (closely related knob controlling the same communication path) is already in CONFIG_DB. Splitting related knobs across CONFIG_DB and constants.yml would be confusing.
 
Note that SYSTEM_DEFAULTS isn't operator-facing — there's no config CLI command to modify it. By default, these entries don't exist in CONFIG_DB (disabled), and the code treats missing entries as disabled. They're only added deliberately via provisioning or sonic-db-cli when a deployment is ready to enable them.

This PR broke marvell-prestera armhf build:

```
5.087 Package libgoogle-perftools4 is not available, but is referred to by another package.
5.087 This may mean that the package is missing, has been obsoleted, or
5.087 is only available from another source
5.087 However the following packages replace it:
5.087   libgoogle-perftools4t64
5.087 
5.091 E: Package 'libgoogle-perftools4' has no installation candidate
```

From the pipeline build for this PR:

```
[ building ] [ target/docker-fpm-frr.gz ] 
[ cached ] [ target/docker-fpm-frr.gz ]
```

Thanks for reporting this @saiarcot895.

**Root cause:** On Trixie armhf, the Debian time64 transition renames `libgoogle-perftools4` → `libgoogle-perftools4t64`. On amd64/arm64, the old name still works as a virtual/transitional package, but on armhf it's completely gone — only `libgoogle-perftools4t64` is available.

**Why CI didn't catch it:** The marvell-prestera-armhf build had a cache hit for `docker-fpm-frr.gz` (as shown in your logs: `[ cached ] [ target/docker-fpm-frr.gz ]`), so the Dockerfile was never actually rebuilt on that arch during CI.

**Planned fix:** Change `libgoogle-perftools4` → `libgoogle-perftools4t64` in `dockers/docker-fpm-frr/Dockerfile.j2`. Since docker-fpm-frr is always Trixie-based (`DOCKER_SWSS_LAYER_TRIXIE`), this should work across all archs — `sonic-slave-trixie/Dockerfile.j2` already uses `libgoogle-perftools4t64` without any arch conditional.

Do you have any other suggestions or concerns with this approach? I'll put up a fix PR shortly.

**`sonic-buffer-queue.yang` has the same missing leafref issue**

`VOQ_BUFFER_QUEUE_LIST.port` is a plain `string` with no leafref to PORT — structurally identical to the `VOQ_QUEUE_LIST.ifname` gap this PR fixes. The non-VOQ `BUFFER_QUEUE_LIST` already has a leafref, so GCU will have the same blind spot for buffer queue entries on VOQ chassis when a port is removed.

`sonic-system-port.yang` (`SYSTEM_PORT_LIST.ifname`) is in a similar situation, though arguably more nuanced since it holds entries for all linecards.

Would be worth applying the same union+must pattern to `sonic-buffer-queue.yang` as a follow-up.

Revised the fix per @-discussion: instead of gating SAI out of the bookworm pass (which broke `syncd` configure since gearbox dockers `gbsyncd-{broncos,milleniob,agera2}` depend on `$(SYNCD)`), `sai-xgs.mk` now uses **dual versions**:

- `BLDENV=trixie` → SAI **15.2.0_GA** (this is what the final image consumes; `IMAGE_DISTRO=trixie`)
- `BLDENV=bookworm` → SAI **14.3.0_GA** (transitional, satisfies syncd link-time `-lsai` for the gearbox container syncd binary)

The bookworm syncd binary is only baked into `gbsyncd-*` containers, which dynamically resolve `-lsai` against `libsai{broncos,milleniob,agera2}` at runtime — so the link-time SAI version is functionally irrelevant. This will be removed once broadcom is fully migrated to trixie.

> Team, Please check my comments below
> 
> High — Bugs to Fix Before Merge
> 
> 1. platform_device_register_resndata — wrong error check (pddf_multifpgapci_i2c_xiic_module.c)
> 
> platform_device_register_resndata() returns ERR_PTR(errno) on failure, never NULL. The guard if (!platform_device) only catches NULL and would store a poison pointer into i2c_xiic_privdata->platform_devices[index], triggering a crash on the next platform_device_unregister call.
> 
> // BUG — does not catch ERR_PTR returns struct platform_device *platform_device = platform_device_register_resndata(...); if (!platform_device) { ret = -ENOMEM; // also wrong — should use PTR_ERR(platform_device) goto free_clk_lookup; } Should be:
> 
> struct platform_device *platform_device = platform_device_register_resndata(...); if (IS_ERR(platform_device)) { ret = PTR_ERR(platform_device); goto free_clk_lookup; }
> 
> 2. pddf_multifpgapci_remove — pci_irq_vector() called beyond allocated count (pddf_multifpgapci_driver.c)
> 
> The teardown loop iterates MAX_NUM_MSI_VECTORS (3) unconditionally, but pci_irq_vector(dev, i) will BUG/WARN if i >= num_msi_vectors (the MSI-X/MSI table only has as many entries as were allocated):
> 
> // BUG — i can exceed pci_privdata->num_msi_vectors for (unsigned i = 0; i < MAX_NUM_MSI_VECTORS; ++i) { if (pci_privdata->msi_domain_irq_chip_data[i]) regmap_del_irq_chip(pci_irq_vector(dev, i), // ← fault if i >= num_msi_vectors pci_privdata->msi_domain_irq_chip_data[i]); } Fix: gate on pci_privdata->num_msi_vectors:
> 
> for (unsigned i = 0; i < pci_privdata->num_msi_vectors; ++i) { if (pci_privdata->msi_domain_irq_chip_data[i]) regmap_del_irq_chip(pci_irq_vector(dev, i), pci_privdata->msi_domain_irq_chip_data[i]); } for (unsigned i = 0; i < MAX_NUM_MSI_VECTORS; ++i) kfree(pci_privdata->regmap_irqs[i]);
> 
> 3. Clock unregistered before child platform devices are torn down (pddf_multifpgapci_driver.c)
> 
> In pddf_multifpgapci_remove, clk_hw_unregister_fixed_rate is called, but the xiic platform devices registered by pddf_multifpgapci_i2c_xiic_module are only freed when that module is unloaded (via pddf_multifpgapci_i2c_xiic_detach). If the PCI driver is removed while the xiic module is still loaded, the xiic platform devices will still hold a reference to the now-freed clk_hw, causing a use-after-free when the xiic driver accesses the clock.
> 
> The fix is to ensure the protocol module detach callbacks (pddf_multifpgapci_i2c_xiic_detach) are invoked before the clock is freed in pddf_multifpgapci_remove. Check whether the existing delete_fpga_data_node path triggers detach, or add an explicit ordered teardown.
> 
> Medium — Should Fix 4. irq_line_mask type mismatch in dev_operation (pddf_multifpgapci_driver.c)
> 
> register_msi_domain takes unsigned long long irq_line_mask, but in dev_operation it is read with sscanf(..., "%x", &irq_line_mask) where irq_line_mask is unsigned int. The %x conversion into unsigned int then widened to unsigned long long on the call. For 32-bit masks this works accidentally, but upper bits are silently dropped for wider masks and the types don't match:
> 
> // dev_operation: unsigned int msi_domain, irq_line_mask, unmask_reg, status_reg; // unsigned int int sscanf_result = sscanf(buf, "register_msi_domain %u %31s %x %x %x", &msi_domain, irq_chip_name, &irq_line_mask, ...); // then passed to: register_msi_domain(..., (unsigned long long)irq_line_mask); // implicit upcast Either change the signature to unsigned int irq_line_mask (consistent with 32-bit max reg_width), or use unsigned long long + %llx in sscanf.
> 
> 5. should_fpga_use_msi — KeyError if dev_attr is absent (pddfparse.py)
> 
> def should_fpga_use_msi(dev) -> bool: if "use_msi" in dev: check_fpga_version_cmd = dev["dev_attr"].get("check_fpga_version_cmd", "") If dev_attr is missing from the JSON, this raises KeyError instead of gracefully returning False. Fix:
> 
> check_fpga_version_cmd = dev.get("dev_attr", {}).get("check_fpga_version_cmd", "") 6. input_clk_hz == 0 — silent misconfiguration when not specified (pddf_multifpgapci_driver.c)
> 
> If input_clk_hz is omitted from the PDDF JSON, pddfparse.py defaults it to 0. A clk_hw_register_fixed_rate with 0 Hz succeeds, but the downstream xiic driver will derive wrong timing and likely hang or mis-program the SCL divisor. At minimum add a warning; if MSI is enabled and clk_hz == 0, fail loudly:
> 
> if (n_msi_vectors > 0 && clk_hz == 0) { printk(KERN_ERR "%s: [%s] input_clk_hz must be > 0 for MSI path\n", MULTIFPGA, **FUNCTION**); ret = -EINVAL; goto free_bars; }

3. This is irrelevant now that the clk is removed as a requirement of `fpgapci_init` and in pddf-device.json. But, for reference, before `clk_hw_unregister_fixed_rate` in is called `pddf_multifpgapci_remove`, `delete_fpga_data_node` invokes the detach function of pddf_multifpgapci_i2c_xiic_module, which also calls `platform_device_unregister`
4. Done. Changed the type of `irq_line_mask` to unsigned int
5. Done. `should_fpga_use_msi` returns False if `dev_attr` does not exist.
6. I have removed `input_clk_hz`. The `clk_hw` and `clk_lookup` are no longer needed or used given the i2c-xiic patch in http://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-linux-kernel/blob/master/patches-sonic/0016-i2c-xiic-skip-input-clock-setup-on-non-OF-systems.patch
